issueTitle: Jitsi: agent meeting participation - dispatch, sidecar, and MCP tools
objective: Implement meeting-aware Krate agent dispatch so Jitsi-capable agents can join active meetings through AgentDispatchRun meeting context, a Kubernetes sidecar, in-meeting MCP tools, auto-dispatch from meeting templates, and web UI links.

planningNotes.processLibraryRequestedPathStatus: active process library reported by babysitter process-library:active --json; repo-local .a5c/process-library is absent in this checkout
planningNotes.existingIssueCommentObservation: Issue comments mention earlier planning PR #670 and open planning PR #715. This process should still read current issue/PR state before execution and not assume prior artifacts are authoritative.

acceptanceCriteria: AgentStack or AgentDefinition exposes Jitsi capability fields without duplicating #620 identity contracts.
Manual dispatch accepts meetingRef only for Jitsi-capable stacks or definitions and rejects inactive, missing, or unauthorized meetings.
AgentDispatchRun records meetingRef and redacted/non-secret meetingContext; raw Jitsi JWT values are not persisted in long-lived CRD status/spec, UI models, comments, or logs.

The runtime Job manifest includes a jitsi-agent-sidecar, JITSI_ROOM_URL, JITSI_JWT, JITSI_ROOM_ID, JITSI_PARTICIPANT_NAME, JITSI_AUDIO_MODE, JITSI_CHAT_MODE, AGENT_SOCKET_PATH, main-agent socket env, and a shared emptyDir socket volume only when meetingContext is present.
The existing workspace volume behavior still works with the sidecar/socket volume.

Eight in-meeting MCP tools exist and are gated by role/capabilities: send_chat_message, get_meeting_transcript, get_participant_list, raise_hand, share_screen, invite_to_meeting, start_recording, react.
Meeting-template autoJoin or autoInvite dispatches agents when a meeting starts and is idempotent.
Web dispatch surfaces show a Meeting field only for Jitsi-capable stacks, forward meetingRef, show run detail meeting links, and show participant dispatch-run links.
Focused core, CLI/MCP, and web tests pass, along with repository metadata/build verification.
securityInvariants: Raw JWT values must never appear in AgentDispatchRun status, JitsiMeeting status intended for UI, labels, annotations, PR body, issue comments, or logs.
JWT delivery to the sidecar must be runtime-only or secret-backed and covered by tests.
MCP meeting tools must deny actions outside the run meeting context and outside the configured role/capabilities.
Moderator-only actions include invite_to_meeting and start_recording.
Read-only chat configurations must deny send_chat_message while still allowing get_meeting_transcript where permitted.
Screenshare must be denied unless jitsiConfig capabilities allow screenshare share.

allowedBreakpoints: Dependency contract is too unstable to proceed without choosing compatibility shims or waiting.
Final quality gate fails after automated remediation attempts.
